case_title: FREDERICK THOMAS SEHER VS. GEORGE VALVERDE, DIRECTOR STATE OF CALIFORNIA,
cause_of_action: WRITS OF MANDATE OR PROH., CERTI., ETC./ADMIN. AGEN

docket_entries.description: NTC OF LODGING OF ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD FILED BY PETITIONER SEHER, FREDERICK THOMAS

LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES' PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS CONTINUED TO DECEMBER 4, 2012 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE PETITIONER THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. PETITIONER MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF HIS MOVING PAPERS AND THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD TO DEPT. 302 WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE NO LATER THAN 4PM NOVEMBER 16, 2012. THE COURT HAS A COPY OF RESPONDENT'S OPPOSITION. JUDGE: MARLA MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976
THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: ON WRIT OF MANDATE (SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT)
OPENING BRIEF OF PETITIONER IN SUPPORT OF WRIT OF MANDATE FOR ORDER SETTING ASIDE SUSPENSION OF DRIVING PRIVILEGE FILED BY PETITIONER SEHER, FREDERICK THOMAS
MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-09-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES' PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS DENIED. OFFICER CAVALLERO HAD THE AUTHORITY TO DETAIN PETITIONER UNDER PENAL 830.8(A)(4) AND 830.8(B). THE LENGTH OF THE DETENTION WAS REASONABLE UNDER THE CIRCUMSTANCES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077.

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Dec-04-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Kapsack, Esq., 510.645.0027, for Petitioner Frederic T. Seher.
John T. McGlothlin, Deputy Attorney General, 510.622.2224, for Respondent Department of Motor Vehicles.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: RESPONDENT DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES' Petition For Writ Of Mandate is denied. Officer Cavallero had the authority to detain petitioner under Penal 830.8(a)(4) and 830.8(b). The length of the detention was reasonable under the circumstances. Order signed in open Court. Judge: A. James Robertson II, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.

Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Nov-09-2012 Continued To Dec-04-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:
RESPONDENT DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES' Petition For Writ Of Mandate is continued to December 4, 2012, to give petitioner the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Petitioner must deliver courtesy copies of his moving papers and the administrative record to Department 302 with a cover letter stating the new hearing date no later than 4pm November 16, 2012. The Court has a copy of respondent's opposition. Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 = (302/MJM)
